meaning: adj.被风吹的,被风吹斜的,(头发)风飘型的
examples: Silt and windblown soil buried the site even deeper.
Interbedded with the salt were thin layers of what appeared to be windblown silt.
The geologic story the Opportunity read there always spoke of an ancient martian wasteland of windblown dunes pocked by the occasional acid-laced puddle.
Elsewhere in Amarillo, iReporter Michael Jaison shot video of the windblown snow outside his house.
The fabled golden wealth of Timbuktu turned out to be sand and a few windblown mud huts.
